issues.description: The new test asserts `event_id is not None`, but `EventStore.emit()` has return type `-> None` and never returns a value. This test will fail at runtime with `AssertionError` because `event_id` will always be `None`.
issues.suggested_fix: Either update `EventStore.emit()` to return a row ID (e.g., `return cursor.lastrowid`) and change its signature to `-> int | None`, or remove this test and the `event_id` assertion entirely if returning an ID is not intended.

issues.description: Capturing `event_id = store.emit(...)` in `test_emit_writes_event` but never using or asserting on `event_id` is dead code. If this is preparation for a future change to `emit()`'s return type, it's premature; if accidental, it's confusing.
issues.suggested_fix: Either remove the `event_id =` assignment (keep bare `store.emit(...)` as before), or add an assertion on the returned value if emit's signature has been changed.

issues.description: Weakening `assert len(rows) == 1` to `assert len(rows) >= 1` reduces test precision. This test emits exactly one event into a fresh temporary database, so there should be exactly one row. The looser assertion would mask bugs where spurious rows are inserted.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the original `assert len(rows) == 1` to maintain strict verification of the single-event case.

issues.description: `assert any(True for r in rows if r['task_id'] == '99')` followed by `assert len(rows) > 0` is redundant. If `any()` passes, `len(rows) > 0` is guaranteed. The assertions also check weaker properties than they could — neither verifies exactly one row was written.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace both assertions with `assert len(rows) == 1` and `assert rows[0]['task_id'] == '99'` for a more precise and non-redundant check.

issues.description: Even if `emit()` were updated to return an ID, this test never correlates the returned `event_id` with the stored row (e.g., verifying the row's `id` column matches `event_id`). The test name promises more than it delivers.
issues.suggested_fix: After fixing the return type issue, assert that the returned ID matches the stored row: `assert rows[0]['id'] == event_id`.

comprehensive_reviewer.summary: The diff adds a new test (`test_emit_returns_event_id`) and modifies an existing test (`test_emit_writes_event`). The critical issue is that the new test asserts `emit()` returns a non-None event ID, but `EventStore.emit()` is typed `-> None` and never returns a value — this test will fail at runtime. The existing test modification weakens a previously strict row-count assertion from `== 1` to `>= 1` without justification, and captures a return value that is never used. The new test also has redundant assertions (`any()` + `len > 0`) and doesn't correlate the returned ID with the stored row, testing less than its name claims.
